subject: UX & Research
title: The Amazon $0.01 Pricing Bug
description: A fast breakdown of The Amazon $0.01 Pricing Bug for builders.

hook.text: Sellers listed products on Amazon for $0.01 because the price field defaulted to zero when left blank.

buildup.text: Repricing tools used Amazon's API to adjust prices automatically. An empty field became one cent.

discovery.text: Customers bought expensive electronics for a penny. Sellers lost thousands before the error was caught.

twist.text: The fix was a minimum price floor in the form. A simple validation rule prevented millions in losses.

climax.text: Empty fields should never silently default to extreme values. Validate boundaries on every numeric input.

punchline.text: Validate input boundaries. Empty fields shouldn't mean 'zero.'

quiz.question: How could Amazon have prevented the $0.01 pricing bug?
options: Adding minimum price validation instead of silently defaulting empty fields to zero
Requiring sellers to use a calculator
